title: Immunohistochemistry for Mitochondrial Marker
author: Jennifer Rutkowsky
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.56ng9de

steps.title: Frozen tissue sections (10μM) on slides...
steps.instruction: Frozen tissue sections (10μM) on slides (tissues embedded in OCT) are then fixed in 4% paraformaldehyde for 5 minutes. a. Alternatively paraffin embedded sections are deparaffinized through 3 100% xylene 3 min wash b. Sections were rehydrated through 100%, 95%, 70%, 50%, and 30% then into deionized water (3min each). c. Antigens are unmasked by steaming in Sodium citrate for 10 min.

steps.title: Wash three times for ten minute in PBS
steps.instruction: Wash three times for ten minute in PBS

steps.title: Blocked and permiablize with Block/Perm Solution for 30 minutes
steps.instruction: Blocked and permiablize with Block/Perm Solution for 30 minutes.

steps.title: Incubate with anti-mitochondria antibody [MTC02] diluted...
steps.instruction: Incubate with anti-mitochondria antibody [MTC02] diluted 1:500 in Block/Perm overnight at 4°C.

steps.title: Wash three times in Block/Perm for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Wash three times in Block/Perm for 10 minutes

steps.title: Incubated in goat anti-mouse IgG Alexa...
steps.instruction: Incubated in goat anti-mouse IgG Alexa 555 (1:750) for 1 hour at room temperature.

steps.title: Washing three times in Block/Perm and briefly in PBS
steps.instruction: Washing three times in Block/Perm and briefly in PBS

steps.title: Washed with PBS three times for 10 minutes each wash
steps.instruction: Washed with PBS three times for 10 minutes each wash

steps.title: Coverslip mounted with Prolong gold antifade reagent
steps.instruction: Coverslip mounted with Prolong gold antifade reagent.
